subsystem.function: Tilt axis with bearing seats, servo linkage, rotation clearance arc

instruction: Design only this subsystem: P0 plan, part_tree children, 12-24 coordinate-bearing geometry_ops. Every evidence_feature must appear. Do not modify other subsystems or the global envelope.

criteria.good: P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
criteria.bad: P0 lists features but geometry_ops are generic boxes at the origin.
criteria.loop_feedback: Map every P0 feature target into a named part_tree child and at least one geometry_op id or target.

criteria.good: Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume.
criteria.bad: Geometry is stacked at [0,0,0] or lacks coordinates.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add coordinate-bearing geometry_ops across upstream/downstream/side/service regions instead of stacking features.

criteria.good: Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, cylinder, chamfer, fillet, pattern, and engrave where appropriate.
criteria.bad: Primary output is a rectangular box with shallow labels.
criteria.loop_feedback: Replace box-only geometry with interface bosses, service pockets, drilled holes, channels, ribs, and labels.

criteria.good: Motion claims are grounded in a named mechanism type (four-bar linkage, cam, rack-and-pinion, lead screw, ratchet, geneva) with its pivot/axis geometry present.
criteria.bad: Design claims motion ('arm rotates', 'finger flexes') but no pivot holes, gear teeth reference articles, cam profiles, or linkage bars exist in geometry.
criteria.loop_feedback: Name the mechanism type behind every motion claim and add its pivot holes, links, or cam/gear reference article geometry.

criteria.good: Tilt axis, bearing seats, servo/linkage access, and nacelle rotation clearance are visible.
criteria.bad: Nacelle is a generic motor pod without tilt-axis evidence.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add tilt-axis bearing seats, servo linkage cover, rotation clearance arc, and nacelle interface datums.

criteria.good: Wing spar socket, fuselage spine, and payload/battery service paths are separate and inspectable.
criteria.bad: Wing/fuselage is a blended block with no interface features.
criteria.loop_feedback: Expose wing spar sockets, fuselage spine datum, payload bay, and slide-in battery service feature.

criteria.good: Output remains academic study structureup, avoiding operational flight tuning or control-law details.
criteria.bad: Design claims BVLOS capability, flight readiness, or actionable aircraft tuning.
criteria.loop_feedback: Frame VTOL features as academic study structure assembly evidence, not flight-qualified or operational design.

criteria.good: Tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings, and motor/servo modules remain replaceable through access covers.
criteria.bad: Nacelle attaches by an undefined blend; motor and servo are entombed with no removal path or load fitting.
criteria.loop_feedback: Add named tilt-bearing fittings into the wing spar path and removable motor/servo covers on each nacelle.

criteria.good: A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed.
criteria.bad: No datum reference — assembly alignment is uncontrolled.

criteria.good: Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm.
criteria.bad: Interfaces have no tolerance or fit specification.

criteria.good: cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step.
criteria.bad: No assembly order — build sequence is ambiguous.

criteria.good: Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, pocket, drill, subtract. Subtractive features (drill/pocket/channel) and finishing (fillet/chamfer) both present.
criteria.bad: Only basic box/cylinder ops. Missing subtractive or finishing features typical for this seed.

criteria.good: Dimensions match tiltrotor scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia. Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible.
criteria.bad: Dimensions are placeholder (all 10mm) or unrealistic for the subsystem scale.

citable_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook SP-2016-6105 Rev2
1800 Mechanical Movements, Devices and Appliances (Hiscox, 1899, public domain)

boundary_note: Subsystem scale: 200-1500mm wingspan, 50-300mm fuselage dia. Environment: outdoor flight, -20 to +50C, rain/dust/salt. Design loads: rotor thrust 20-200N, tilt servo 5-30Nm, 3g maneuvering. Mechanism: include joint travel range, preload, and cycle life in cad_brief.

operability_profile.operating_principle: VTOL aircraft architecture that transitions thrust vector through nacelle tilt axes while preserving wing load paths, slack-loop harnesses, and control authority.
